strengths: Nearly 20 years of engineering experience spanning web, embedded, and distributed systems, with current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq — directly on-target for the agentic-AI architecture mandate

Proven high-scale distributed-systems depth: at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic he built low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and real-time data, matching the event-streaming and real-time inference requirements

Relevant fintech and product-ownership background: regulated German investment platform work at RIDE Capital, multi-country algorithmic pricing at Optilyz, plus founding and operating GISCollective, an event-driven platform he architected end-to-end on Kubernetes
concerns: No explicit executive or VP/CTO title; career evidence is at senior/staff IC level, so the 10-to-30+ team-scaling track record required by the role is not directly demonstrated
Agentic-AI experience is recent and tooling-oriented; the resume does not show multi-agent systems operating in production with direct financial consequences
Specific fintech-infrastructure depth (credit-risk systems, payment flows, exposure modeling) and LLM fine-tuning/evaluation methodology are not evidenced on the resume
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo pairs rare breadth — nearly two decades across backend, embedded, mobile, and platform engineering — with the exact technical center of gravity this role demands: he is currently building agentic-AI infrastructure and MCP tooling, and he has operated microservices at millions of requests per second, giving him credible fluency in event-driven architecture, low-latency systems, and observability. His fintech exposure at RIDE Capital and pricing-engine work at Optilyz, combined with founder experience running GISCollective and a long record of mentorship, TDD, and engineering-culture building (workshops, dojos, courses), map well onto the role's demands for architectural leadership, disciplined shipping culture, and developing technical talent. While his formal people-scaling record is lighter than the spec, his founder autonomy, craftsmanship-driven leadership style, and directly relevant agentic and distributed-systems depth make a strong case that he can grow into the executive scope while immediately owning the hardest technical problems.
